title: Users may be unable to install user targeted apps that have been made available in the Intune Company Portal
impact: Users may be unable to install user targeted apps that have been made available in the Intune Company Portal.

updates.content:
Title: Users are unable to install user targeted apps that have been made available in the Intune Company Portal

User impact: Users are unable to install user targeted apps that have been made available in the Intune Company Portal.

Current status: We're investigating a potential issue with Intune and checking for impact to your organization. We'll provide an update within 30 minutes.

Title: Users may be unable to install user targeted apps that have been made available in the Intune Company Portal

User impact: Users may be unable to install user targeted apps that have been made available in the Intune Company Portal.

More info: Users may see the app download stuck as "Download Pending".

Current status: We're reviewing whether any recent changes could be contributing to the install issues, which will help us determine our next troubleshooting steps.

Scope of impact: This issue may potentially impact any user attempting to install user targeted apps in the Intune Company Portal. This information may be updated as our investigation continues.

Next update by: Tuesday, April 7, 2026, at 5:30 AM UTC

Title: Users may be unable to install user targeted apps that have been made available in the Intune Company Portal

User impact: Users may be unable to install user targeted apps that have been made available in the Intune Company Portal.

More info: Users may see the app download stuck as "Download Pending".

Current status: We're continuing our analysis of recent changes as well as collected diagnostic logs from affected devices to help identify the source of the issue.

Scope of impact: This issue may potentially impact any user attempting to install user targeted apps in the Intune Company Portal. This information may be updated as our investigation continues.

Next update by: Tuesday, April 7, 2026, at 11:00 AM UTC

Title: Users may be unable to install user targeted apps that have been made available in the Intune Company Portal

User impact: Users may be unable to install user targeted apps that have been made available in the Intune Company Portal.

More info: Users may see the app download stuck as "Download Pending". 
 
Current status: We've identified that a recent service update introduced a regression that prevented app installations from progressing as expected which changed how a required configuration file was packaged, resulting in the file not being available and preventing app downloads from completing. We’ve deployed a service update to address the issue, and the fix is gradually rolling out across affected environments that corrects how the Intune management agent handles a required configuration file during upgrades, which restores app installation functionality. 
 
Scope of impact: Impact is specific to some users attempting to install user targeted apps in the Intune Company Portal. This information may be updated as our investigation continues.  
 
Start time: Thursday, April 2, 2026, at 3:30 AM UTC
 
Root cause: A recent service update introduced a regression that prevented app installations from progressing as expected which changed how a required configuration file was packaged, resulting in the file not being available and preventing app downloads from completing. 
 
Next update by: Tuesday, April 7, 2026, at 1:00 PM UTC

Current status: We’ve begun a staged release of the fix, with the update first being introduced in environments where validation has completed, while additional testing continues in higher impact environments to help ensure a safe and reliable rollout.

Title: Users may be unable to install user targeted apps that have been made available in the Intune Company Portal

User impact: Users may be unable to install user targeted apps that have been made available in the Intune Company Portal.

More info: Users may see the app download stuck as "Download Pending". 
 
Current status: We’re continuing the fix deployment, with approximately 80% of affected devices already upgraded, while monitoring deployment progress and remaining device convergence as we work toward completion of the fix rollout by Thursday, April 9, 2026.  
 
Scope of impact: Impact is specific to some users attempting to install user targeted apps in the Intune Company Portal. This information may be updated as our investigation continues.

Title: Users may be unable to install user targeted apps that have been made available in the Intune Company Portal

User impact: Users may be unable to install user targeted apps that have been made available in the Intune Company Portal.

More info: Users may see the app download stuck as "Download Pending." While we work on our long-term fix, impacted users should update their Intune clients from version 1.101.103.0 to 1.101.105.0 in order to receive this short-term fix and bypass impact.

Current status: We’re continuing to monitor our short-term fix deployment as it saturates throughout the affected infrastructure while validating its efficacy through analyzing service health telemetry. In tandem, we're deploying our long-term fix as version 1.101.109.0 in order to fully remediate impact for this issue. We'll provide a timeline for this long-term fix saturation and full impact remediation as one becomes available. 

Scope of impact: Impact is specific to some users attempting to install user targeted apps in the Intune Company Portal. This information may be updated as our investigation continues.

Start time: Thursday, April 2, 2026, at 3:30 AM UTC

Root cause: A recent service update introduced a regression that prevented app installations from progressing as expected which changed how a required configuration file was packaged, resulting in the file not being available and preventing app downloads from completing.

Next update by: Thursday, April 9, 2026, at 1:00 AM UTC

Current status: We're rolling out our long-term fix through version 1.101.109.0 of the Intune client and anticipate this rollout may be complete by our next scheduled update, at which point we'll monitor and test to confirm full remediation.

Current status: The rollout of our solution was slightly delayed and we're now monitoring its deployment. Our updated deployment timeline estimates that it will have completed and for impact to be remediated by our next scheduled communication update.

Title: Users may be unable to install user targeted apps that have been made available in the Intune Company Portal

User impact: Users may be unable to install user targeted apps that have been made available in the Intune Company Portal.

More info: Affected users may observe that app installations in the Intune Company Portal are stuck in a “Download Pending” state. A fix is currently being deployed, and impacted devices will receive an Intune client upgrade to version 1.101.109.0.
Users who attempted to install user targeted apps while their device was running a version of the IME impacted by this issue and whose group memberships were modified by an administrator before the fixed IME version was deployed, may continue to experience the issue. We’re actively working on an additional fix to remediate this subset of users.

 Current status: Although it’s taking longer than we previously anticipated, the fix has nearly completed saturating throughout the affected environment, thus we expect some users may see remediation at this time. We'll continue to monitor the remainder of the deployment, and we anticipate that it will be completed by the time of our next scheduled update.
 
Scope of impact: Impact is specific to some users attempting to install user targeted apps in the Intune Company Portal. This information may be updated as our investigation continues.
 
Start time: Thursday, April 2, 2026, at 3:30 AM UTC
 
Root cause: A recent service update introduced a regression that prevented app installations from progressing as expected which changed how a required configuration file was packaged, resulting in the file not being available and preventing app downloads from completing.
 
Next update by: Friday, April 10, 2026, at 9:30 PM UTC

Title: Users may be unable to install user targeted apps that have been made available in the Intune Company Portal

User impact: Users may be unable to install user targeted apps that have been made available in the Intune Company Portal.

More info: Affected users may observe that app installations in the Intune Company Portal are stuck in a “Download Pending” state. A fix is currently being deployed, and impacted devices will receive an Intune client upgrade to version 1.101.109.0.
Users who attempted to install user targeted apps while their device was running a version of the IME impacted by this issue and whose group memberships were modified by an administrator before the fixed IME version was deployed, may continue to experience the issue. We’re actively working on an additional fix to remediate this subset of users.
 
Current status: We've completed the deployment of our fix but review of service health telemetry identified a subset of affected users that remain impacted due to user targeted app installation attempts during the impact period. We've developed a supplemental fix to restore functionality for the remaining impacted users, which we've begun deploying to the affected environment. We anticipate the supplemental fix will complete deployment and fully remediate the impact by our next scheduled communications update.
 
Scope of impact: Impact is specific to some users attempting to install user targeted apps in the Intune Company Portal. This information may be updated as our investigation continues.
 
Start time: Thursday, April 2, 2026, at 3:30 AM UTC
 
Root cause: A recent service update introduced a regression that prevented app installations from progressing as expected which changed how a required configuration file was packaged, resulting in the file not being available and preventing app downloads from completing.
 
Next update by: Monday, April 13, 2026, at 9:30 PM UTC
